title: Avoid This Masseter Botox Pattern
excerpt: The actual resource muscle is inserted into the fascia around the master when you're treating the master if you get a little bit superior and a little bit medial then you can affect the resource muscle and that causes sometimes an even more upsetting asymmetry of smiling and that…

segments.text: the actual resource muscle is inserted into the fascia around the master when you're treating the master if you get a little bit superior and a little bit medial then you can affect the resource muscle and that causes sometimes an even more upsetting asymmetry of smiling and that that's basically because you're relaxing a little muscle that pulls the corner of the mouth when you do a big smile so what can we do to avoid that so yeah one of the problems i realized with this is if you google a master reduction botox pattern or injection points for toxin you get a very common description that if you actually look at the lines which i'm sure we get up on the screen for you you'll see that the line that is often used draws a line um right to the oral commissure and if you look at the top kind of right hand side of where that line is inferior to it you're actually very close to the insertion point of resorius and i know from training clinicians that when you see them in practice trying to implement these things

implement these things it's only half a centimeter to centimeter different and you're right in the resorius so i don't like using that line in fact i use a different line which is to really line up with the mental crease so you're treating the lower third of the master muscle but my boundary is the mental crease and that takes you away from that insertion point of resorius which i think is likely to reduce the number of side effects of this smile of the smile being affected with a toxin treatment in master so when you're injecting the masseter what do you do to reduce that risk apart from using a different boundary which means i'm less likely to inject near that insertion point of resorius the other thing is really important when injecting the master to avoid that particular side effect as well as another side effect is to make sure that you're deep enough so if the actual resource muscle is inserted into the fascia around the master so it's relatively superficial um and so if we make sure we're deep that's the second thing you can do so we need to be inferior to it and too deep to actually hit the resorius is an effective way of reducing that risk

transcript: the actual resource muscle is inserted into the fascia around the master when you're treating the master if you get a little bit superior and a little bit medial then you can affect the resource muscle and that causes sometimes an even more upsetting asymmetry of smiling and that that's basically because you're relaxing a little muscle that pulls the corner of the mouth when you do a big smile so what can we do to avoid that so yeah one of the problems i realized with this is if you google a master reduction botox pattern or injection points for toxin you get a very common description that if you actually look at the lines which i'm sure we get up on the screen for you you'll see that the line that is often used draws a line um right to the oral commissure and if you look at the top kind of right hand side of where that line is inferior to it you're actually very close to the insertion point of resorius and i know from training clinicians that when you see them in practice trying to implement these things implement these things it's only half a centimeter to centimeter different and you're right in the resorius so i don't like using that line in fact i use a different line which is to really line up with the mental crease so you're treating the lower third of the master muscle but my boundary is the mental crease and that takes you away from that insertion point of resorius which i think is likely to reduce the number of side effects of this smile of the smile being affected with a toxin treatment in master so when you're injecting the masseter what do you do to reduce that risk apart from using a different boundary which means i'm less likely to inject near that insertion point of resorius the other thing is really important when injecting the master to avoid that particular side effect as well as another side effect is to make sure that you're deep enough so if the actual resource muscle is inserted into the fascia around the master so it's relatively superficial um and so if we make sure we're deep that's the second thing you can do so we need to be inferior to it and too deep to actually hit the resorius is an effective way of reducing that risk
